text: In the first moment, I feel the lively press of bodies in a crowded gathering—there is a hum of conversation, laughter, and the sound of cans being opened. I’m aware of the physical closeness and the almost chaotic convergence of people and movement. The closeness is both comforting and slightly overwhelming. In the second moment, I find myself in a more contemplative space, gazing at text and visuals on vibrant walls. The words invite reflection—suddenly, I am drawn inward, appreciating the sensory and philosophical interplay between outer experience and inner thought. I feel at once present with others and deeply alone in my thoughts.
